prose:
Frontier-orbital calculation on O₂ (dioxygen, triplet ground state) performed at the DFT level using the B3LYP functional with the def2-TZVP basis set (chemkit tier 'standard'), in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 3 (open-shell UKS), using PySCF as the backend. The geometry was taken as-is from the user-supplied .xyz file (single-point; no re-optimization). SCF converged cleanly in 7 cycles. Integrity verdict: trustworthy: true, all checks passed. No warnings were reported by the engine.

Key results:
- HOMO energy: −8.8739616 eV (degenerate; reflects the two singly-occupied π* orbitals of triplet O₂)
- LUMO energy: −3.5859031 eV (also degenerate, LUMO and LUMO+1)
- HOMO–LUMO gap (headline value): 5.2880586 eV
- Total electronic energy: −150.38872 Hartree (−4092.2855 eV)

Koopmans' theorem estimates (orbital-based): vertical IP = 8.8740 eV, vertical EA = 3.5859 eV, electronegativity = 6.2299 eV, chemical hardness = 2.6440 eV, softness = 0.37821 eV⁻¹, electrophilicity index = 7.3396 eV.

CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --charge 0 --mult 3 --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/oxygen/oxygen.xyz
